mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates compress short-term real yields and signal an accommodative stance that pushes term premiums lower, resulting in a decline in the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any rolling 2-year window, the 20-day yield change on days with FEDFUNDS below 1% is not consistently negative (median change >= 0).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above a low baseline signals a deterioration in the labor market that increases recession probabilities and flight-to-safety demand for Treasuries, pushing the 10-year yield lower.
failure_condition: In the 20 trading days after each UNRATE > 4.0% release, the median yield change is not negative, or the proportion of negative outcomes is not above 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a broad economic contraction, reinforcing expectations of further easing and disinflation, which lowers long-term Treasury yields.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and HOUST post negative 3-month growth
failure_condition: In periods where both series decline year-over-year, the 60-day yield change does not have a negative median or is not lower than in periods without joint declines (Mann-Whitney p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a high-inflation regime, elevated consumer price levels erode the real return of nominal Treasuries, causing investors to demand higher yields, which drives the 10-year yield upward.
failure_condition: In periods with CPIAUCSL > 230, the 20-day yield change is not positive on average (mean <= 0) over any consecutive 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth signals robust aggregate demand and wage pressures, leading to higher inflation expectations and a rise in the 10-year yield over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any 1-year period, the 5-day yield change after payroll releases in the top quartile of PAYEMS levels does not show a positive median (median <= 0).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling sharply below a low threshold indicate a severe housing downturn that depresses economic activity and fuels safe-haven demand, lowering the 10-year Treasury yield.
failure_condition: In the 20 trading days after HOUST readings below 1500k, the median yield change is not negative, or the hit rate of negative days is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the central bank cuts interest rates while consumer prices continue to climb, the combination of accommodative policy and rising inflation expectations creates a stagflation scare, pushing long-term yields higher.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines and CPIAUCSL rises over a 3-month window
failure_condition: In periods when both conditions hold, the 60-day yield change does not exceed the unconditional median yield change by at least 10 basis points (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During NBER-defined recessions, a sustained fall in retail sales confirms deepening demand destruction, which forces the Fed to ease further and reduces inflation pressures, resulting in a lower 10-year yield.
failure_condition: During official recession months (NBER chronology), the 60-day yield change is not negative on average, or the slope coefficient from regressing yield changes on RSAFS growth is not significantly positive (p>0.10).
